# Israel AD Fails 80% Iran Missiles; China on US bombing; UK / Telegraph proxy frame — Alexander Mercouris (digest)

**Source:** YouTube — **Alexander Mercouris** (episode title as given by operator). **The Duran** / associated channels referenced at close (Locals, Rumble, X, Substack).

**Watch URL:** *Add exact `https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=…` when pinned.*

**Air date (spoken):** 2026-03-30 (Monday).

**Ingested:** 2026-03-30 (operator paste).

**Scope:** [work-strategy](../../../../docs/skill-work/work-strategy/README.md) upstream — monologue + cited secondary sources (**Military Watch**, **Haaretz**, **Global Times**, **G7**, **Bloomberg**, **Daily Telegraph**). **Not** companion Record. Verify all **battle** and **intercept** claims independently.

---

## Perceiver — neutral summary (≤200 words)

Mercouris (2026-03-30) centers **Military Watch**’s summary of **Haaretz** that **~80%** of **Iranian** missiles reach **Israel** as **U.S./Israeli** **air defenses** weaken—**Qatar/UAE** **radar** losses and **Hezbollah** pressure cited; he accepts **Haaretz** as fact-reliable despite **Netanyahu** criticism, hedging to a **majority** penetration rate. He ties **Patriot/THAAD** reputation to **Ukraine** **intercept** narratives and argues **Russian** missiles are no weaker than **Iranian**. He describes **escalation**: **Pakistani** mediation talk, **Strait of Hormuz** leverage, **Israeli** strikes on **Iranian steel**, reported **Iranian** counterstrikes on **Kuwait** (**desalination**), **Trump** **Financial Times** remarks (**Kharg**, **oil**), **~50,000** **U.S.** personnel, vague **war aims**, **special-forces**–heavy force structure vs **line** **infantry**, and **U.S.** **ground** war without **air superiority** (since **1943** frame). **Global Times** editorial (People’s Daily system) demands **ceasefire**, blames **Washington**, warns **civilian infrastructure** spiral and **Houthi** **Red Sea** risk; he contrasts **G7** (**27 Mar**, France) **wording** with **Chinese** bluntness and critiques **European** **energy** **preparedness**; cites **$200/bbl** industry talk (**Bloomberg**). **Con Coughlin** (**Telegraph**) **Russia**–**Iran** **proxy** frame — Mercouris counters with **Ukraine** **aid** symmetry. **Long segment:** **Donbas** **tactical** claims (**Konstantinovka** factories, **Lyman**, **Sloviansk**/**Kramatorsk**) — treat as **opinionated** **war** **commentary**, not verified **order of battle**.

Good day. Today is Monday, 30th March, 2026. And before I proceed with this program, let me remind you again to tick the like button and check your subscription to this channel if once you've watched this program, you decide that you like what you've heard and you wish to indicate that fact.
 Now, the single most important piece of news coming out of the Middle East conflict that I saw this morning was one which was actually published about two days ago and which was reported in the Israeli newspaper Haaretz, but which um I'm afraid I missed when it was published. And um this report this was a report which says that 80% of Iranian missiles launched against Israel get through and strike their targets.
And I got this news I saw this news in an in an article that appears in Military Watch magazine. one of this infinity of military magazines that are published in the United States and it says the following. Israeli sources confirm Iranian missile strikes have 80% 80 that's 80% success rates as air defenses falter.
And the article, this is the military watch magazine continues, "The Israeli paper has confirmed that eight out of 10 Iranian missiles launched against Israeli targets are reaching their targets following mounting reports and growing quantities of footage pointing to the failures of Israeli and US ballistic missile defenses.
 The report further noted that success rates have continued to improve as air defenses have become increasingly strained. Israeli analysts observed that contributing factors have included the systematic exhaustion of the air defense network and the destruction of US forward radar systems in allied Arab states such as Qatar and the UAE which have limited the quantities of queuing data that can be provided.
 Sources further observed that mass bombardment by Hezbollah in Lebanon has further strained Israeli and US defenses. Now I have here to add a cautionary note. This article in Military Watch magazine derives from an article in Haaretz. I have not read that article in Haaretz and Haaretz which is a leftist center newspaper in Israel very critical of Prime Minister Netanyahu and of his government and very critical of the foreign policies and security and defense policies the prime minister and his government have followed.
 Well, Haaretz is not a neutral source anyway. However, I have consistently found Haaretz to be a reliable reporter of events in the Middle East and in Israel itself. one has to allow for their biases. But I have never known them to let their biases interfere in reporting important facts of this kind.
 So I am going to ride with this story. I'm going to assume that it is true. Maybe 80% is overstating what is happening. But let us assume that it is 50 plus% or 60% or something like that. A large proportion, probably a majority, maybe the bulk of Iranian missiles are penetrating US and Israeli defenses and are striking their targets in Israel.
 And of course, we've now had strikes on Israel conducted by Hezbollah, as this article in Military Watch magazine says. And there have also been further strikes on Israel carried out by the Houthis in Yemen.  Um, this has also been claimed for US air defenses too. IC missiles, missiles like the Khal and the Tiron. And these claims have been repeated entirely uncritically by the media in the West.  Well, at times I won't deny that it has been the exercise has been a little bit like talking into the wind.  at least when the first Patriot missiles started to be delivered to Ukraine.  a point that somebody rather acidly made on the thread of my previous video, but they are very very good indeed. and that beyond that staffs in Washington and London have become over invested in special forces and in special forces operations and have shown less interest and understanding and given less attention to what to to the nature needs of the more conventional line infantry, the infantry divisions who are the people who must in the end win the wars.

It does seem to me that speaking speaking in Britain and I think the same is true in the United States when I was younger, say in the 60s or the early 70s, special forces units in Britain and the United States formed part of a very very large, very complex uh military system in which there were infantry divisions, there aircraft, aviation, all of these things and every part of it o every part of it complemented and supported the other.
What has happened over the last decades is that all of those other parts have shrunk whilst the special forces have remained at roughly the same level and have if anything grown bigger and have received even more funding. As for reserves, as for husbanding reserves, as we see what all of the Western powers are doing in the face of a looming energy shortage is deplete their reserves as fast as they can to keep oil prices just a little bit lower for just a little bit longer. And we have a situation where the reserves of all of the of all of these things have been depleted to to critical levels. I will not be surprised. some reports of a Russian advance from the south into Oreov itself and even some claims that the Russians have broken into though I've seen no confirmation of this. And it yesterday the Russian newspaper Readovka claimed that the Russians have broken into the central area of Konstantinovka, the industrial sector, and that they're now fighting for control of the factories there.
 The Readovka report reads as follows. The main the week's main success was the eighth guard army breakthrough in the center of Konstantinovkaovka. Assault units have entered the industrial district, securing the Krasni Octi plant and part of the railway station near the metallurgical plant. The Ukrainians were not expecting an attack here.

And um the article continues, the Russian military planned to occupy the industrial zone before the enemy could turn it into a fortified stronghold. from the northeast. the supply right the control of the supply lines will gradually tighten and the point will eventually come at some point in the summer when the collapse takes place we are at that point now the battle for Sloviansks has begun the last phase of the battle of Donbas.
